subject: Computer Networking
title: Browser Caching
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Browser Caching.

hook.text: Visit a site again and it loads faster. Your browser cached the files the first time. No re-download needed.

buildup.text: Cache-Control headers tell the browser how long to keep a file. max-age=3600 means cache for one hour.

discovery.text: ETags let the browser ask: has this file changed? The server responds 304 Not Modified if it hasn't.

twist.text: Aggressive caching causes stale content. Cache-busting adds a hash to filenames so updates are forced.

climax.text: Service workers give you full cache control in JavaScript. You can serve pages even when offline.

punchline.text: Caching speeds up the web. The trick is knowing when to let go.

quiz.question: What HTTP status code means the cached version is still valid?
